# Palette Home

> Free, fully client-side colour wheel and palette generator for home
> decorating, in English and German. Pick a colour and a harmony, or
> extract colours from a photo, and get a decor-ready 60-30-10 palette
> with a live room preview. No accounts, no tracking, no uploads.

URL: https://reckarhor.github.io/palette-home/

## What it does

- Interactive colour wheel with six harmonies: complementary,
  split-complementary, analogous, triadic, tetradic, monochromatic.
- Every palette is mapped to interior-design roles using the 60-30-10
  rule: dominant (walls, large furniture), secondary (sofa, curtains),
  accent (cushions, art, lamps).
- Each swatch shows its LRV (Light Reflectance Value, the 0-100 scale
  printed on paint chips) and a warm/cool undertone badge, plus a
  7-step brightness ladder; clicking a step repaints the palette and
  the room illustration with that exact colour.
- The dominant colour is softened into a wall-ready paint tint,
  adjusted for room light (dim/north-facing, average, bright/south).
- Photo palettes: an uploaded photo of a room, rug or artwork is
  analysed on-device (k-means clustering, nothing uploaded) into base,
  supporting and pop colours; a lightbox lets users click the photo to
  correct any of the three.
- Palettes are encoded in the URL for sharing:
  c = base hex, m = harmony mood, d = room light, b = brightness picks,
  p = photo palette (three hex values).

## Facts

- Free, static site, no backend, no analytics, no cookies.
- Languages: English and German (switcher; defaults to device language).
- Source: https://github.com/reckarhor/palette-home
